data.narrative:
Turion Space is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public-market aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with 4 same-stage, same-sector comparables directly applied.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $75M total-raised proxy as a base and applies a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, yielding $142M. Peer-median analysis references four same-sector, same-stage companies—Aalyria ($1.3B), Stoke Space ($1.0B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—which produce a median valuation of $700M and range from $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as Turion's revenue is not publicly disclosed.

These two methods triangulate to a range of $121M (low) to $805M (high), with a median of $421M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in peer valuations within the Space & Aerospace sector at Series B stage. Key uncertainties include Turion's revenue trajectory, unit economics relative to peers, and whether the company's technical capability or market position justifies placement toward the upper or lower end of peer valuations.
